test_cases.test_case: Email format validation - invalid email format

steps: 1. Enter <invalid email format> in the Email/Username field
2. Enter <valid password meeting policy> in the Password field

test_cases.test_case: Password policy violation - representative (too short / missing required character classes)
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and is presented with the sign-in page (no active user session).
steps: 1. Enter <registered email> in the Email/Username field
2. Enter <password shorter than minimum length or missing required character classes> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Sign In' button
test_cases.expected_result: Password field displays an inline validation error indicating the policy is not met, for example: 'Password must be at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character'; the form does not submit and the user remains on the sign-in page

test_cases.test_case: Server-side authentication failure - incorrect credentials
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and is presented with the sign-in page (no active user session).
<registered email> exists in the registered-user database
steps: 1. Enter <registered email> in the Email/Username field
2. Enter <incorrect password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Sign In' button
test_cases.expected_result: Server rejects credentials; page displays exact error message: 'Incorrect email or password. Please try again.'; the Password field is cleared; no redirect occurs and the user remains on the sign-in page (no session is created)

test_cases.test_case: Precondition violation - authenticated user attempts to access Sign In page
preconditions: User is already authenticated with an active session and has been redirected to Accounts Overview
steps: 1. Navigate to the Sign In page URL (or click a link that would open the sign-in page)
test_cases.expected_result: Access to the Sign In page is blocked due to precondition; the user is redirected to the Accounts Overview and the Sign In form is not displayed

test_cases.test_case: Sign in with a password that is exactly the minimum length and satisfies all character-class requirements
preconditions: A registered user exists with an account whose Email/Username is <registered user email> and whose password meets the stated constraints
Visitor is not authenticated and is on the sign-in page
steps: 1. Enter <registered user email> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a password that is exactly <minimum length 8> and contains at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits and authentication succeeds: the UI flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and redirects to the Accounts Overview page — succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt sign in with a password that is one character shorter than the minimum length
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and is on the sign-in page
steps: 1. Enter <a valid email format> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a password that is exactly <one unit below minimum length (7)> but otherwise contains uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Password field displays an inline error indicating the value is below the minimum allowed length and submission is blocked / error shown.

test_cases.test_case: Password meets length but is missing the required special character
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and is on the sign-in page
steps: 1. Enter <a valid email format> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a password of length >= <minimum length 8> that contains at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one number but contains no special character in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Password field displays an inline error indicating it 'must contain at least one special character' (or equivalent) and submission is blocked / error shown.

test_cases.test_case: Email/Username input with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed on submit
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and is on the sign-in page
steps: 1. Enter <a valid email format with leading and trailing whitespace> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a password that satisfies the stated character and length constraints in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Leading/trailing whitespace is trimmed from the Email_Username value (visible in the input after blur/submit). The system then attempts authentication using the trimmed email; if credentials for the trimmed email exist the sign in succeeds and the UI flashes 'Signed in successfully.' and redirects to Accounts Overview; if not, the failure message 'Incorrect email or password. Please try again.' is shown and the Password field is cleared.

test_cases.test_case: Very long Email/Username input (200+ characters) to check handling/truncation
preconditions: Visitor is not authenticated and is on the sign-in page
steps: 1. Enter <a very long email string (200+ characters)> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter a password that satisfies the stated character and length constraints in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
test_cases.expected_result: Either the UI displays an inline error indicating the email exceeds maximum allowed length and submission is blocked / error shown, or the input is accepted (no client-side error) and the system attempts authentication; the UI then displays the server response (on success: 'Signed in successfully.' and redirect; on failure: 'Incorrect email or password. Please try again.' and Password cleared).

test_cases.test_case: Browser Back after a successful sign-in then immediate resubmit from the sign-in page
preconditions: A registered user exists with an account whose Email/Username is <registered user email> and whose password meets the stated constraints
Visitor is not authenticated and is on the sign-in page
steps: 1. Enter <registered user email> in the Email_Username field
2. Enter the correct password for that user in the Password field
3. Click Sign In
4. Wait for the UI to flash 'Signed in successfully.' and redirect to the Accounts Overview page
5. Using the browser, press the Back button
6. On the sign-in page shown after navigating back, click Sign In without changing any fields
test_cases.expected_result: Navigating back does not produce a second successful creation event or duplicate success flash. Either the second submission is prevented by the UI (submit disabled) or it re-uses the existing authenticated session such that no additional 'Signed in successfully.' flash is shown and the user remains signed in with Accounts Overview accessible; visually there is no duplicate success notification or duplicate session indicator — user remains authenticated. (Visible: only one 'Signed in successfully.' flash was shown and Accounts Overview remains accessible.)

steps: 1. From a browser session where the user is not logged in, navigate to the Payments page URL or click the Payments item in the left-hand navigation
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Login page; Payments page is not displayed and no payment form is accessible (e.g., login form is visible).

test_cases.test_case: Submit Pay with mismatched Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number
preconditions: Authenticated user on the account dashboard with at least one active source account
steps: 1. Log in as an authenticated user and navigate to the account dashboard
2. Click the Payments link in the left-hand navigation to open the Bill Payment form
3. Enter <payee name> into the Payee Name field
4. Enter <payee street> into the Street Address field
5. Enter <city> into the City field
6. Enter <state> into the State field
7. Enter <zip> into the ZIP Code field
8. Enter <phone number> into the Phone Number field
9. Enter <account A> into the Payee Account Number field
10. Enter <different account B> into the Confirm Account Number field (value not matching Payee Account Number)
11. Enter <valid payment amount within available funds> into the Payment Amount field
12. Select a Source Account with sufficient funds
13. Click the Pay button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Confirm Account Number field with the message: 'Account numbers do not match'. Form is not submitted; no confirmation reference is returned; account balances remain unchanged; the form remains editable.

test_cases.test_case: Payment Amount rejects non-numeric input
preconditions: Authenticated user on the account dashboard with at least one active source account
steps: 1. Log in and navigate to the Payments page
2. Fill Payee Name with <valid payee name>
3. Fill Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number with a matching valid account number
4. Enter <non-numeric input> into the Payment Amount field
5. Select a Source Account with sufficient funds
6. Click the Pay button
test_cases.expected_result: Payment Amount field displays an inline validation error indicating the value must be numeric (e.g., 'Enter a valid amount' or similar). The form does not submit; no confirmation reference is returned; payment is not executed and account balances are unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Submit Pay with amount exceeding available funds (Insufficient funds)
preconditions: Authenticated user on the account dashboard with at least one active source account whose available balance is less than the intended payment amount
steps: 1. Log in and navigate to the Payments page
2. Fill Payee Name with <valid payee name>
3. Fill Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, and Phone Number with valid values
4. Enter a matching value into Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number
5. Enter <amount exceeding available balance> into the Payment Amount field
6. Select the Source Account whose available balance is less than <amount entered>
7. Click the Pay button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error is displayed indicating insufficient funds (error message example: 'Insufficient funds'). The form does not submit; no confirmation reference is returned; account balances remain unchanged; the form remains editable.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to submit Pay when user has no active Source Account
preconditions: Authenticated user on the account dashboard with NO active source accounts
steps: 1. Log in as an authenticated user whose profile has no active source accounts and navigate to the Payments page
2. Observe the Source Account dropdown (it is empty or lists no selectable accounts)
3. Fill Payee Name, address fields, Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number (matching), and Payment Amount with valid values
4. Attempt to click the Pay button
test_cases.expected_result: Pay action is blocked: the Source Account control shows an inline indicator that no active source accounts are available (e.g., inline error on Source Account or disabled dropdown) and either the Pay button is disabled or clicking Pay displays an inline error on the Source Account field such as 'No active source accounts available'. The form does not submit; no confirmation reference is returned; account balances remain unchanged.

test_cases.test_case: Submit with all required fields empty

5. Click the Change Password button.
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on Current Password, New Password, and Confirm New Password indicating each is required; the Change Password action is blocked; no password change occurs and the user remains on the Security Settings form.

test_cases.test_case: Confirm New Password does not match New Password

steps: 1. Navigate to Account Dashboard → Security Settings while authenticated.

4. Enter <different value new password B> in the Confirm New Password field.
5. Click the Change Password button.

test_cases.test_case: Incorrect Current Password verified on submit

test_cases.test_case: New Password violates strong-password policy
preconditions: User is logged in with an active authenticated session and has access to the account dashboard/navigation.
steps: 1. Navigate to Account Dashboard → Security Settings while authenticated.

5. Click the Change Password button.
test_cases.expected_result: New Password field displays an inline validation error indicating the password does not meet the system's strong-password policy (e.g., minimum length/complexity); the Change Password action is blocked; no password change occurs.

preconditions: User is not authenticated (logged out)
steps: 1. Open the application Support Center page URL or click the 'Support Center' item in the navigation without logging in
test_cases.expected_result: Access is blocked and the user is redirected to the Login page; Support Center forms (Secure Message and Schedule Callback) are not visible

test_cases.test_case: Send Message blocked when required Message Body is left blank
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
steps: 1. Ensure the Message Body field in the Secure Message form is left blank
2. Click the 'Send Message' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Message Body field indicating it is required; the form does not submit and no ticket ID is returned or displayed

test_cases.test_case: Request Callback blocked when all required callback fields are empty
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
steps: 1. Clear the Preferred Date field in the Schedule Callback form (leave it blank)
2. Clear the Phone Number field (remove the prefilled value)
3. Click the 'Request Callback' button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on the Preferred Date and Phone Number fields indicating they are required; the callback request is not submitted and no confirmation email is sent

test_cases.test_case: Send Message blocked when attachment file type is invalid
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
steps: 1. Upload a file of <unsupported file type> into the Attachment field of the Secure Message form
2. Click the 'Send Message' button
test_cases.expected_result: Attachment field displays an inline validation error indicating the file type is invalid; the message is not sent and no ticket ID is returned

test_cases.test_case: Send Message blocked when Subject violates length validation
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
steps: 1. Enter <subject exceeding maximum allowed length> into the Subject field
2. Enter <valid message body> into the Message Body field
3. Click the 'Send Message' button
test_cases.expected_result: Subject field displays an inline validation error indicating the subject length validation failed; the message is not sent and no ticket ID is returned

test_cases.test_case: Request Callback blocked when Preferred Date is earlier than next business day
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
steps: 1. Enter <date earlier than next business day> into the Preferred Date field of the Schedule Callback form
2. Click the 'Request Callback' button
test_cases.expected_result: Preferred Date field displays an inline validation error stating it 'must be at least the next business day'; the callback request is not submitted and no confirmation email is sent

test_cases.test_case: Request Callback blocked when Phone Number format is invalid
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
steps: 1. Edit the Phone Number field in the Schedule Callback form to <invalid phone number format>
2. Click the 'Request Callback' button
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number field displays an inline validation error indicating the phone number format is invalid; the callback request is not submitted and no confirmation email is sent

test_cases.test_case: Schedule callback with Preferred Date exactly equal to the next business day (boundary pass)
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
Schedule Callback form is visible
steps: 1. Click into the Preferred Date field
2. Select <next business day> in the date picker
3. Enter a valid selection in Reason For Call
4. Enter or accept the prefilled Phone Number
5. Enter a Preferred Time Window
6. Click Request Callback
test_cases.expected_result: Request Callback submits successfully; confirmation message 'Callback request submitted.' is shown and the UI displays a visible confirmation (e.g., confirmation panel) indicating an email confirmation will be sent — overall submission succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Schedule callback with Preferred Date one business day before the next business day (boundary fail)
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
Schedule Callback form is visible
steps: 1. Click into the Preferred Date field
2. Select <one business day before the next business day> in the date picker
3. Enter a valid selection in Reason For Call
4. Enter or accept the prefilled Phone Number
5. Enter a Preferred Time Window
6. Click Request Callback
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked / error shown: an inline validation error appears on the Preferred Date field (e.g., 'Preferred date must be at least the next business day') and the Request Callback action is blocked.

test_cases.test_case: Secure message with Message Body containing only whitespace (rich-text trimmed to empty) — presence requirement edge
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
Secure Message form is visible
steps: 1. Enter a valid Category selection
2. Enter a Subject (leave to default or fill as needed)
3. Focus the Message Body rich-text editor
4. Enter a sequence of whitespace characters only (e.g., spaces/newlines) into the Message Body
5. Click Send Message
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is blocked / error shown: an inline validation error appears for Message Body indicating it must be present (e.g., 'Message Body is required') and the Send Message action is blocked.

test_cases.test_case: Secure message with long rich-text message (200+ characters) — long-text handling
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
Secure Message form is visible
steps: 1. Enter a valid Category selection
2. Enter a valid Subject
3. Enter a long message of 200+ characters into the Message Body rich-text editor
4. (Optional) Attach no file
5. Click Send Message
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; 'Message sent successfully.' confirmation is shown with a ticket ID and the saved message detail displays the full long text entered (submission succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Subject containing emoji and unicode characters — special-character handling
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
Secure Message form is visible
steps: 1. Enter a Subject containing emoji and non-ASCII unicode characters
2. Enter a valid Category selection
3. Enter a valid Message Body
4. Click Send Message
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; 'Message sent successfully.' confirmation is shown with a ticket ID and the saved subject displayed in the confirmation/detail view contains the emoji/unicode characters as entered (submission succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Attachment with a valid file type but uppercase extension (file type validation case handling)
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
Secure Message form is visible
steps: 1. Enter a valid Category selection
2. Enter a valid Subject
3. Enter a valid Message Body
4. Upload an attachment whose extension matches an allowed type but is uppercase (e.g., file name with uppercase extension)
5. Click Send Message
test_cases.expected_result: Attachment type validation accepts the file and the Send Message action completes successfully; 'Message sent successfully.' confirmation is shown with a ticket ID and the confirmation UI shows the uploaded file name (submission succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Phone Number prefilled but edited with leading/trailing whitespace — trimming behavior
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
Schedule Callback form is visible
Phone Number field is prefilled
steps: 1. Focus the Phone Number field
2. Edit the Phone Number by adding leading and trailing whitespace around the prefilled number
3. Enter a valid selection in Reason For Call
4. Select <next business day> in Preferred Date
5. Enter a Preferred Time Window
6. Click Request Callback
test_cases.expected_result: Request Callback submits successfully; confirmation message 'Callback request submitted.' is shown and the saved/confirmation view displays the phone number trimmed of leading/trailing whitespace (submission succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid re-submission after successful Send Message using browser Back — duplicate-submission prevention
preconditions: User is authenticated and on the Support Center page
Secure Message form is visible
steps: 1. Enter a valid Category selection
2. Enter a valid Subject
3. Enter a valid Message Body
4. Click Send Message
5. On the success confirmation view, note the displayed ticket ID
6. Use the browser Back button to return to the form
7. Attempt to click Send Message again with the same form content
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission attempt is blocked / error shown: the application does not create a duplicate ticket. The UI either shows the original confirmation/detail (preventing re-submit) or displays an inline duplicate-submission warning and no second ticket ID is created (only the original ticket exists).

module_context.summary: The Login module provides a credential form that validates Email/Username and Password against the registered-user database and handles successful or failed authentication responses.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is the initial sign-in entry point shown to visitors before reaching the Accounts Overview dashboard; upon successful authentication it redirects users into the app where the left-hand navigation (Accounts Overview, Open New Account, Transfer Funds, etc.) becomes available. It sits at the start of the user session lifecycle, gating access to all subsequent modules.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Visitor is not authenticated and is presented with the sign-in page (no active user session).

module_context.summary: A pre-authentication sign-up form that collects personal, contact, and credential data with client-side formatting and validation and creates a new customer account upon successful submission.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is part of the pre-login flow reachable from the sign-in page via the Register link and precedes access to the post-login left-hand navigation (Accounts Overview, Open New Account, Transfer Funds, etc.). It operates before account access and hands off to the Login module after successful account creation.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Unauthenticated visitor who selected the Register link from the sign-in page and intends to create a new account.

module_context.summary: Displays a personalized dashboard listing all customer accounts with masked account numbers, account details, status badges, and a total balance footer.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is the default dashboard view reached immediately after a successful login and is accessible via the left-hand navigation as Accounts Overview among options like Open New Account, Transfer Funds, and Security Settings. It serves as the primary landing page showing an overview of a customer's accounts and balances within the application's main lifecycle after authentication.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer session with permission to view the customer's account data.

module_context.summary: A form-based feature that creates a new Checking or Savings account by selecting an account type, specifying an initial deposit and funding source, and performing real-time validations on inputs and balances.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under 'Open New Account' after successful sign-in, this module is part of the account onboarding flow in the dashboard and, on success, redirects users back to the Accounts Overview to show the new account.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated and on the dashboard with at least one existing funding account available for the initial deposit

module_context.summary: Module for initiating fund transfers either between the user's ParaBank accounts or to an external account, including validation of amounts and account numbers and displaying success or error messages.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under 'Transfer Funds' after successful sign-in and arrival at the Accounts Overview/dashboard; it is used during regular account operations to move money within the user's accounts or to external accounts and occurs after account selection in the session lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has at least one active Checking or Savings account available to select as a source; for external transfers the user may provide an external account number to confirm.

module_context.summary: Processes bill payments by collecting payee and payment details, validating entries (including matching account numbers and available funds), executing the payment, returning a confirmation reference, and updating account balances.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is the application's Bill Pay screen accessible from the left-hand navigation after successful log-in (from the account dashboard). It is part of post-login account management features alongside Transfer Funds, Open New Account, and Payments-related tasks.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated user on the account dashboard with at least one active source account (and sufficient funds for the intended payment)

module_context.summary: Provides an interface to apply for Personal, Auto, or Home loans with validation of amounts, down payments, collateral, and a simulated credit decision.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation under "Request Loan" after successful sign-in, this module is part of the post-login financial services allowing users to initiate new loan requests alongside other actions like Transfer Funds and Open New Account. It sits in the application lifecycle at the point where authenticated customers request credit and receive an immediate simulated approval or denial outcome.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has at least one existing account eligible to be selected as a collateral account with sufficient displayed balance

module_context.summary: Editable customer profile form for viewing and updating personal contact details with validation and success/error feedback.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as 'Update Contact Info' (item ⑥) after successful login and landing on the account dashboard; it is part of the post-authentication account management flow for maintaining personal information.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and on the account dashboard with an existing customer profile whose contact fields are available to be loaded and edited.

module_context.summary: Provides card issuance requests and card-control settings so customers can request new debit or credit cards and update spending limits, travel notices, and card status.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation after successful log-in via the "Manage Cards" menu option, this module is part of post-login account management functionality alongside Accounts Overview, Open New Account, Transfer Funds, and other account services. It is used during the account lifecycle when customers need to request new cards or manage existing card controls.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated customer session with at least one existing account eligible for card linkage

module_context.summary: Provides a portfolio snapshot and workflows for executing same-day fund trades and creating recurring investment plans, with input validation and confirmation messages.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation (Investments, item ⑧) after successful login and landing on the account dashboard; it is the application area for managing brokerage-style holdings and scheduling contributions as part of account management features.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in customer session with at least one linked funding/destination account and investment privileges (existing holdings or funding account available).

module_context.summary: Provides generation of account statements for a selected account and period and management of e-statement (paperless) preferences including email validation.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the left-hand navigation as the Account Statements option (menu item ⑨) after successful sign-in; it is part of the post-login account management features alongside Accounts Overview, Transfer Funds, and other account services. This module is used when users need transaction histories or to update paperless delivery settings within their account lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is signed in and has at least one active account associated with their profile

module_context.summary: Provides a form for authenticated users to change their account password by verifying the current password, enforcing a strong-password policy, and updating credentials with validation feedback.
module_context.where_it_fits: Available from the left-hand navigation after successful login under Security Settings (reachable from the Account Dashboard / Accounts Overview). It is part of the account management and security lifecycle, used to maintain credential integrity for signed-in customers.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in with an active authenticated session and has access to the account dashboard/navigation.

module_context.summary: Support Center provides authenticated customers with a secure message form (subject, category, rich-text message, optional attachment) and a schedule-callback form (reason, preferred date/time window, phone) with inline validation and success confirmations including ticket IDs and email confirmation.
module_context.where_it_fits: Available from the left-hand navigation menu after login as the 'Support Center' (item 11), this module is part of post-authentication customer support features accessed from the account dashboard and sits alongside other account management functions like Security Settings and Account Statements.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is an authenticated/customer who has successfully logged in and reached the account dashboard
